description:
Goal: define the function property that replaces `defguard`-style special casing: a dispatch-pure helper can be reified into `PatternGuardExpr` plus nested dispatch plans.

Change:
- Add a Compiler2 job/fact that reads `FunctionDefined` plus frozen namespace and either publishes a compiler-owned guard reification or emits fatal diagnostics through telemetry.
- Accept single-clause and multi-clause helpers, transitive dispatch-pure helper calls, destructuring heads, captures, and cycle detection.
- Define the accepted subset precisely as “reifiable into `PatternGuardExpr` and nested `PatternDispatchPlan`”, not merely “referentially transparent”.
- Publish the shared planner shape directly; do not invent a Compiler2-only helper-dispatch mirror structure.
- Cache revisions so redefining a helper retracts and republishes only dependent guard/dispatch facts.

Why:
- “Pure” alone is too broad for matcher/codegen reuse; the useful property is reifiable guard semantics.
- This removes the need for explicit `defguard` semantics without replacing it with a new pile of Compiler2-only helper machinery.

Checks:
- Compiler2 integration tests cover single-clause, transitive, multi-clause, destructuring, captured-arg, cycle-rejection, and impure-rejection helper cases.
- Tests assert through telemetry/job effects and published fact behavior, not world internals.

Non-goals:
- No function-entry dispatch yet.
- No body-site (`case`/`with`/`receive`) dispatch planning yet.
- No compatibility shim that re-lowers helper bodies through old whole-program machinery.
title: Compiler2 dispatch: reify dispatch-pure helper functions
